ogTitle: Gunnedah vs Gunning Gap: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gunnedah and Gunning Gap.
heading: Gunnedah vs Gunning Gap — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gunnedah, New South Wales and Gunning Gap, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gunnedah has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Gunning Gap (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gunnedah or Gunning Gap safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gunnedah has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Gunning Gap scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gunnedah scores 31/100 for bushfire risk. Gunning Gap scores 63/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gunnedah or Gunning Gap?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gunnedah has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
